chunks.text: Hi guys, welcome to another live session by SimpleLearn.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: I would like to thank you all for actively participating in these sessions on YouTube
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: and Facebook.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: Today, we will understand how to perform stock market prediction using machine learning.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: Now let's wait for a while for people to join in.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: We have a really exciting session today.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: Meanwhile, please subscribe to our SimpleLearn YouTube channel and stay updated with all
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: the latest trending technologies.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: Also, if you have any questions for us during the course of this video, please put it in
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: the chat section.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: Our team of experts will help you solve your queries.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: I can see a lot of people have joined in without any further delay.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: Let's begin.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: So, what really is stock market?
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: A stock market is a place or a public market where you can buy and sell shares for publicly
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: listed companies.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: The stocks which are also known as equities represent ownership in the company.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: The stock exchange is the mediator that allows the buying and selling of shares.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: Amsterdam stock exchange is the oldest stock exchange that were established in 1602.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: The top stock exchanges in the United States are the New York Stock Exchange, NASDAQ, American
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: Stock Exchange and Chicago Board Operations Exchange.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: In India, we have the Bombay Stock Exchange or the BSC that was established in 1875 and
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: its ACS first stock exchange.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: We also have the National Stock Exchange or NSE.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: Now, let's understand the importance of stock market.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: Stock markets help companies to raise capital.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: If stock markets did not exist, then companies would have to resort to borrowing loans from
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: the banks to raise money for expansion.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: This would be a trouble for the company as they would have to repay the loans with interest.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: With stock markets, companies have the free hand to create an initial public offering and
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: raise large amounts of cash without having to worry about repayment.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: Another reason why the stock market is important is it helps generate personal wealth.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: For an individual investor, the stock market provides a platform to invest your income
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: and earn a share of the company's profit.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: Stock markets serve as an indicator of the state of the economy, that is, the stock market
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: serves as a barometer for the economy.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: A rise of fall in the prices of shares indicates what cycle the economy is in, such as a recession
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: or a boom.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: The stock market is considered to be one of the most widely used sources for people to
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: invest money in.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: Investors are always looking to invest in companies with high growth potential.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: If the stock market is performing well, this increases investment from local investors.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: It also attracts foreign direct investment as people abroad invest in the local stock
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: exchange.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: With that, let's now understand stock market prediction.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: Stock market prediction helps you determine the future value of company stock and other
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: financial instruments traded on an exchange.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: The whole idea of predicting stock prices is to gain significant profits.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: Predicting how the stock market will perform is a difficult task to do.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: There are a lot of other factors involved in the prediction such as physical and psychological
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: factors, rational and irrational behaviour, etc.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: All these factors combine to make share prices dynamic and volatile.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: This makes it very difficult to predict stock prices with high accuracy.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: So we will use machine learning techniques to predict the stock prices of two different
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: companies, Tesla and Google.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: We will be using linear regression algorithm and long short term memory networks for building
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: our prediction models.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: So first, let's understand the basics of linear regression.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: Linear regression is a very popular statistical technique used for solving machine learning
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: problems.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: It is a supervised learning algorithm for predicting the output of a continuous target
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: variable.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: It can be used to predict the total revenue of a company or the total units expected
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: to be sold of a certain product.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: It can also be used for weather prediction and stock price prediction.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: Here is an example to predict the total number of ice creams a vendor or a company can sell
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: based on the temperature of the day.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: You can see the green dots represent the actual data points.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: So the x-axis has the input variable that is temperature and the y-axis has the target
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: variable that is unit sold.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: Linear regression uses the linear equation formula y is equal to mx plus c where m is
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: the slope of the line, c is the intercept.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: We find the coefficients and plot the regression line.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: So in this graph, the red line is a regression line.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: The best fit line should have the least squared distance between the original data points and
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: the predicted values.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: Now let's see how to predict the stock prices of Tesla using the linear regression model.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: So this is the csv data set that we will be using for our prediction.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: We have information from 29 June 2010 till 15 May 2019.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: Here open column means the price at which a stock started trading when the market opened
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: on a particular day.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: The close column refers to the price of an individual stock when the stock exchange closed
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: market for the day.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: It represents the last buy sell order executed between two traders.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: The high column is the highest price at which a stock traded during a period.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: The low is the lowest price of the period.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: Same is the total amount of trading activity during a period of time.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: The adjusted closing price is a calculation adjustment made to the stock's closing price.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: It is more complex and accurate than the closing price.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: The adjustment made to the closing price depicts the true price of the stock because the outside
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: factors could have altered the true price.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: Now we will understand about long short term memory networks.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: So LSTMs are a type of recurrent neural networks for learning long term dependencies.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: It is commonly used for processing and predicting on the basis of time series data.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: From the image on the left, you can see LSTMs have a chain like structure.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: Instead of having a single neural network layer, there are four interacting layers communicating
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: with each other in a very special way.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: Now LSTMs work in a three step process.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: The first step in LSTM is to decide which information to be omitted from the cell in
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: that particular time step.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: It is decided by the help of a sigmoid function.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: It looks at the previous state that is h t minus 1 and the current input state that is
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: x t and computes the function.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: In the second layer, there are two parts.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: One is the sigmoid function and the other is the tanh function or the hyperbolic tangent
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: function.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: In the sigmoid function, it decides which values to let through, that is 0 or 1.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: The hyperbolic tangent function gives the weightage to the values which are passed deciding
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: their level of importance from minus 1 to plus 1.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: In the third step, it decides what will be your final output.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: First, it will run a sigmoid layer which decides which parts of the cell state make
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: it to the output.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: Then we put the cell state through the tanh function to push the values to be between
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: minus 1 and plus 1 and multiply it by the output of the sigmoid gate.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: Now, let's go to our Jupyter notebook and implement a long short term memory network
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: to predict the stock prices of Google.
chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning

chunks.text: Before that, let me first show you the data set that we will be using.

text: Hi guys, welcome to another live session by SimpleLearn. I would like to thank you all for actively participating in these sessions on YouTube and Facebook. Today, we will understand how to perform stock market prediction using machine learning. Now let's wait for a while for people to join in. We have a really exciting session today. Meanwhile, please subscribe to our SimpleLearn YouTube channel and stay updated with all the latest trending technologies. Also, if you have any questions for us during the course of this video, please put it in the chat section. Our team of experts will help you solve your queries. I can see a lot of people have joined in without any further delay. Let's begin. So, what really is stock market? A stock market is a place or a public market where you can buy and sell shares for publicly listed companies. The stocks which are also known as equities represent ownership in the company. The stock exchange is the mediator that allows the buying and selling of shares. Amsterdam stock exchange is the oldest stock exchange that were established in 1602. The top stock exchanges in the United States are the New York Stock Exchange, NASDAQ, American Stock Exchange and Chicago Board Operations Exchange. In India, we have the Bombay Stock Exchange or the BSC that was established in 1875 and its ACS first stock exchange. We also have the National Stock Exchange or NSE. Now, let's understand the importance of stock market. Stock markets help companies to raise capital. If stock markets did not exist, then companies would have to resort to borrowing loans from the banks to raise money for expansion. This would be a trouble for the company as they would have to repay the loans with interest. With stock markets, companies have the free hand to create an initial public offering and raise large amounts of cash without having to worry about repayment. Another reason why the stock market is important is it helps generate personal wealth. For an individual investor, the stock market provides a platform to invest your income and earn a share of the company's profit. Stock markets serve as an indicator of the state of the economy, that is, the stock market serves as a barometer for the economy. A rise of fall in the prices of shares indicates what cycle the economy is in, such as a recession or a boom. The stock market is considered to be one of the most widely used sources for people to invest money in. Investors are always looking to invest in companies with high growth potential. If the stock market is performing well, this increases investment from local investors. It also attracts foreign direct investment as people abroad invest in the local stock exchange. With that, let's now understand stock market prediction. Stock market prediction helps you determine the future value of company stock and other financial instruments traded on an exchange. The whole idea of predicting stock prices is to gain significant profits. Predicting how the stock market will perform is a difficult task to do. There are a lot of other factors involved in the prediction such as physical and psychological factors, rational and irrational behaviour, etc. All these factors combine to make share prices dynamic and volatile. This makes it very difficult to predict stock prices with high accuracy. So we will use machine learning techniques to predict the stock prices of two different companies, Tesla and Google. We will be using linear regression algorithm and long short term memory networks for building our prediction models. So first, let's understand the basics of linear regression. Linear regression is a very popular statistical technique used for solving machine learning problems. It is a supervised learning algorithm for predicting the output of a continuous target variable. It can be used to predict the total revenue of a company or the total units expected to be sold of a certain product. It can also be used for weather prediction and stock price prediction. Here is an example to predict the total number of ice creams a vendor or a company can sell based on the temperature of the day. You can see the green dots represent the actual data points. So the x-axis has the input variable that is temperature and the y-axis has the target variable that is unit sold. Linear regression uses the linear equation formula y is equal to mx plus c where m is the slope of the line, c is the intercept. We find the coefficients and plot the regression line. So in this graph, the red line is a regression line. The best fit line should have the least squared distance between the original data points and the predicted values. Now let's see how to predict the stock prices of Tesla using the linear regression model. So this is the csv data set that we will be using for our prediction. We have information from 29 June 2010 till 15 May 2019. Here open column means the price at which a stock started trading when the market opened on a particular day. The close column refers to the price of an individual stock when the stock exchange closed market for the day. It represents the last buy sell order executed between two traders. The high column is the highest price at which a stock traded during a period. The low is the lowest price of the period. Same is the total amount of trading activity during a period of time. The adjusted closing price is a calculation adjustment made to the stock's closing price. It is more complex and accurate than the closing price. The adjustment made to the closing price depicts the true price of the stock because the outside factors could have altered the true price. Now before I begin the demo, I want to tell you that if you want to get a copy of the source code files and the data sets that I will be using in this session, then please put your email IDs in the chat section of the video. Also, please subscribe to our channel and hit the bell icon to stay updated with all the latest trending technologies. So here is my Jupyter notebook where I have implemented my linear regression code. So I will run through each cell of code and explain what it does. First, we will import the necessary libraries for building our model. So I am importing pandas, numpy and macplotlib for numerical computation, data manipulation and data visualization. We will also import the Plotly library for building our graphs. Now if you are using Plotly for the first time, you will have to install it and then import it onto Jupyter. Let me run this cell. So I have successfully imported all my libraries. Next, I load the tesla.stoprise data set using the pandas library and the read underscore cse function. I will also give the location where my csv data set is present. Let me import the data set. I will hit shift enter. So now I have successfully imported my data set. Now let's see my data set. For that I will use the head function, I will load tesla.head. So this will return the top 5 rows from the data set. In the next cell, I am using the info function to find the total rows and columns in my data set. The info function will also return the data type of the variables and check if there are any null values. The UO. So we have total 7 columns and there are 2193 entries. Here you can see the data type of each column and each column has non null values. Moving ahead, in the next step, we are converting the date column into date time format using pandas library and 2 underscore date time function. Up next, let's do some exploratory data analysis and get more insights from our data. I am using the print function and a formatted string literal that is f. These strings contain replacement fields which are expressions delimited by curly braces. You can see here, I am finding out the data frame contains top prices between what dates and the total number of dates. So the total number of days can be calculated by subtracting the maximum date value and the minimum date value. So let me go ahead and run it. You can see stock prices are from 29th of June 2010 till 15th of March 2019 and the total number of days are 3181 days. Now let's see some summary statistics for our data using the describe function. So the describe function gives you the count, mean, standard deviation, the maximum and minimum from each column, the 25th quartile, the 50th quartile and the 75th quartile. Let me now go ahead and create a box plot to visually check our outliers. I am considering 5 columns, open, high, low, close and adjusted clues. So this is how our box plot looks like. In this cell, we will plot a graph using the plotly library that we have imported. We are setting the layout for our graph using the go layout function and providing a title to the graph. I am also setting my x-axis for giving the title. So the x-axis will have the date column you can see it here. We are also defining the title font, the family, size and color. Similarly, I am providing the layout for the y-axis you can see it here. Finally, I am passing my data list and the layout created to plot variable. Let me go ahead and run it. Now I will plot my graph using the iplot function. There you go. So we have the stock prices of Tesla plotted in this graph. On x-axis, we have the year from 2011 to 2019 and on the y-axis, you have the price which is basically the close column. You can see from the graph that initially during 2011 or 2010, the stock prices were growing a little lower than expected. But towards 2015, 16 and 17 onwards, the stock prices grew higher. Now coming to the most important part, that is to build the linear regression model. So in this cell, I am importing the necessary libraries and their respective functions. First, I am importing the train underscore test underscore split function from the scikit learn library. Next, I am also importing some pre-processing functions like minmax scalar and standard scalar from scikit learn library. Also, for evaluating the model and finding the accuracy, we will use the mean squared error and r squared error from scikit learn matrix library. Let me run it. So we have successfully imported our required libraries. Moving ahead, in the current step, we are splitting the data into training and testing sets. The x variable contains the independent features and the y variable has the dependent variable or the target variable that is close column. I am taking the test data sizes 0.3 or 30% of the total and I am assigning a random state of 101. Let's run it. Okay. Now I will perform feature scaling on the extrained dataset. For that, I am using the standard scalar dot fit function and passing in my extrained data. The standardization of a dataset is a common requirement for any machine learning algorithm. They might behave badly if the individual features are not normally distributed. So that's why feature scaling is important. With that, it's time for us to import the linear regression function from the scikit learn library. Now using a variable lm which stands for linear model, I will declare a linear regression function and then I will use the fit method to pass the training datasets that is x train and y train. So let me run it. Now we have successfully created a linear regression model. Next we will plot the graph for actual and predicted values for the training dataset. Here we will be creating a scatterplot and draw our prediction line. So I have used the go.scatter function. I am using markers for plotting the actual data points and I have used a line to draw the prediction line. Let me run it. Now to plot the graph, I used the iplot function. If I scroll down, you can see on the x axis, we have the day column or the day variable and on the y axis, we have the prices of our stocks. The blue dots represent the actual stock values. If I hover over these blue dots, you can see the actual values and the red line is a predicted regression line. The final step will calculate the scores for evaluating our model. So here we are using two matrix. One is r squared error and mean squared error. For finding the r squared error, we have used r2 underscore score function and I am passing my y train as well as x train using the lm predict function. Similarly, for finding the r squared error for testing dataset, I am using y test and lm predict for x test. Likewise to find the mean squared error value, I have used the y train and y test data along with predicted x train and x test data. Now let me run it. Below you can see the two matrix scores. So our r squared error score is around 0.86 for the training set and it is around the same value of 0.86 for testing dataset and similarly you can see the mean squared error as well. With that, you saw how to predict the tesla stock prices using linear regression model in Python. If you want to get a copy of the source code files and the datasets that I am using in this session, then please put your email IDs in the chat section. We will share the files over email. Also please subscribe to our channel and stay updated with all the latest technologies. Now we will understand about long short term memory networks. So LSTMs are a type of recurrent neural networks for learning long term dependencies. It is commonly used for processing and predicting on the basis of time series data. From the image on the left, you can see LSTMs have a chain like structure. Instead of having a single neural network layer, there are four interacting layers communicating with each other in a very special way. Now LSTMs work in a three step process. The first step in LSTM is to decide which information to be omitted from the cell in that particular time step. It is decided by the help of a sigmoid function. It looks at the previous state that is h t minus 1 and the current input state that is x t and computes the function. In the second layer, there are two parts. One is the sigmoid function and the other is the tanh function or the hyperbolic tangent function. In the sigmoid function, it decides which values to let through, that is 0 or 1. The hyperbolic tangent function gives the weightage to the values which are passed deciding their level of importance from minus 1 to plus 1. In the third step, it decides what will be your final output. First, it will run a sigmoid layer which decides which parts of the cell state make it to the output. Then we put the cell state through the tanh function to push the values to be between minus 1 and plus 1 and multiply it by the output of the sigmoid gate. Now, let's go to our Jupyter notebook and implement a long short term memory network to predict the stock prices of Google. Before that, let me first show you the data set that we will be using. And similarly, to consider only closing column as my training data, I'm using i-lock or index location. Now let me run it. Now let's once again see the data types. Scroll down. Now that you can use the minmax scalar function and give the range. Then I have used fit underscore transform method to transform the values. And then I'm also printing the shape of my training data. Let me run it. Now we will prepare our data for our training. First, we need to prepare our input sequences with 60 time steps of X train data alongside the respective Y train labels. Next, we'll be adding another axis for the batch size since the input for an LSTM network is a 3D tensor. So it will have a sequence length, the time steps and batch size.
